_doc: Authoritative deduped per-character denominators for the Dhamma research program. SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for any density claim. Every per-character magnitude in any study MUST divide a deduped (is_primary) numerator by the matching deduped Mchar below. The un-deduped mula totals (52.3M / 53.5M) are RETIRED. See SKILL rule 8 + the density-claim gate.

snapshot.basis: is_primary = true (the canon is double-ingested SuttaCentral + Chattha-Sangayana; is_primary marks one edition per passage as canonical). Verified against live dhamma-pg.

layer_definition_sql: CASE WHEN work_role='mula' AND work_slug<>'pli-vism' THEN 'canon_mula' WHEN work_slug LIKE '%-attha' OR work_slug='pli-vism' THEN 'atthakatha' WHEN work_slug LIKE '%-tika' THEN 'tika' ELSE 'extra_canonical' END
rules: density(L, S) = (count of is_primary passages in S whose original matches L's pattern, after the homograph/false-friend exclusion mask) / Mchar(S).
is_primary on BOTH numerator and denominator. A deduped numerator over an un-deduped denominator (or vice versa) is the item-5 error class: it silently inflates or deflates the ratio by up to ~2x.
pli-vism (Visuddhimagga) is shelved as work_role='mula' but is COMMENTARY; it is counted as atthakatha here, never as canon.
Never a raw row-ratio headline (commentary was subdivided to ~330-char rows vs the canon's ~2,975-char rows, so a row count mostly measures segmentation). Per-character only.
Where a study used its own stricter regex/sense-coded numerator, recompute with THAT exact pattern + is_primary, not a generic stem, or the count will not reconcile.

retired.un_deduped_mula_53_5M: The pre-2026-06-25 convention. It double-counted the suttas, understating canon density and overstating house (commentary-denser) ratios. Do not use.
retired.note: House studies built before 2026-06-25 (awakening, individual-guidance, heart-base-and-insight, uttarakuru, naga) did NOT use is_primary; their per-character caveats need a proper both-sides recompute (queue item 5).
